executiveSummary:
## Executive Summary: Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

National Medicaid enrollment continues its post-pandemic normalization trajectory, with 74.9 million enrollees across 51 states as of February 2026, representing universal year-over-year declines as states complete their redetermination processes. The enrollment contraction varies significantly by state, ranging from North Carolina's stable enrollment (0.0% change) to Michigan's substantial 7.6% decline. California experienced the largest absolute reduction at 6.8%, while major states including Texas (-3.8%), Florida (-4.2%), and Pennsylvania (-2.9%) showed moderate decreases consistent with expected redetermination impacts. Nationally, per-enrollee spending averaged $2,883 in fiscal year 2024, with total expenditures reaching $215.9 billion and federal contributions comprising approximately 66% of total costs.

Texas maintains its position as the third-largest Medicaid program with 4.0 million enrollees but demonstrates mixed performance relative to peer states. While Texas achieves strong managed care penetration at 93.3% (well above the national average of 83.2%), its per-enrollee costs of $3,014 exceed those of comparable large states like Illinois ($2,719) and North Carolina ($2,623), though remaining below Pennsylvania ($4,363) and Ohio ($3,584). Quality performance presents a particular concern, with Texas scoring 62 out of 100 and ranking 30th nationally—significantly trailing top-performing states like Massachusetts (81) and Minnesota (80). The federal regulatory environment remains active, with recent rulemaking addressing community engagement requirements, state directed payments, and provider enrollment moratoria that may impact state program operations and federal funding structures in the coming fiscal year.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with nearly 4 million enrollees, representing a significant achievement in program scale and managed care implementation. The state demonstrates operational efficiency with per-enrollee costs of $3,014—notably higher than southern peers North Carolina ($2,623) and Florida ($2,036), but substantially below the national average of $2,883. Texas's managed care penetration rate of 93.3% significantly exceeds the national average of 83.2%, indicating strong administrative coordination in delivering services through private plan partnerships. The state's enrollment decline of 3.8% year-over-year aligns closely with national unwinding trends and compares favorably to peer states like California (-6.8%) and Michigan (-7.6%), suggesting relatively stable program management during the post-pandemic transition period.

However, Texas faces persistent quality challenges that demand attention, scoring 62 out of 100 on CMS quality measures—ranking 30th nationally and falling below the national average of 65.6. This moderate performance places Texas behind expansion states but ahead of several non-expansion southern states like Arkansas (54) and South Carolina (53). The quality gap represents a critical area for improvement, particularly given Texas's substantial enrollment base and relatively higher per-enrollee spending compared to regional peers. Key areas to monitor include the state's response to emerging federal regulatory changes around community engagement requirements and managed care payment policies, both flagged in recent Federal Register postings. Texas's ability to leverage its strong managed care infrastructure to drive quality improvements while maintaining cost efficiency will be crucial for program sustainability and beneficiary outcomes.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania Per-Enrollee Spending 51% Above National Average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,363 significantly exceeds the national average of $2,883, indicating potential cost control issues.

alerts.title: Florida Per-Enrollee Spending 29% Below National Average
alerts.description: Florida's per-enrollee spending of $2,036 is substantially lower than the national average of $2,883, suggesting potential underspending or efficiency.

alerts.title: Five States Show Critically Low Quality Scores
alerts.description: Mississippi, Oklahoma, Alabama, South Carolina, and Arkansas have quality scores below 55, indicating severe quality concerns.

alerts.title: Texas Shows Low Quality Despite High Spending
alerts.description: Texas has above-average per-enrollee spending ($3,014) but below-average quality scores (62), suggesting inefficient resource utilization.

alerts.title: CMS Issues Nationwide Provider Enrollment Moratorium
alerts.description: Federal Register documents indicate CMS has announced temporary moratoria on provider enrollment, potentially affecting program access.

executiveInsights.title: New CMS community engagement requirements may impact TX operations
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register shows new Medicaid community engagement requirements for certain individuals becoming active. This could directly affect Texas's 4M enrollees and our contract scope.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Texas HHSC will need implementation support for new federal requirements, creating expansion opportunities for our current contract work.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag to HHSC leadership and assess contract modification opportunities

executiveInsights.title: Texas quality score of 62/100 ranks #30 nationally, below average
executiveInsights.summary: Texas scores 62/100 vs national average of 65.6, ranking 30th of 50 states in moderate tier. This is significantly below top performers like MA (81) and MN (80).
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Poor quality performance creates risk for federal funding and positions Accenture to propose quality improvement initiatives under our contract.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Develop quality improvement proposal for next contract discussion

executiveInsights.title: Texas per-enrollee costs $3,014 exceed national average by $130
executiveInsights.summary: Texas spends $3,014 per enrollee vs national average of $2,883, with total expenditures of $12B. This is higher than similar states like IL ($2,719) and NC ($2,623).
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Above-average costs combined with moderate quality scores indicate efficiency opportunities that Accenture could address through our contract.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Prepare cost optimization analysis for HHSC leadership review

executiveInsights.title: Multiple CMS enrollment moratoria announced affecting provider networks
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register shows nationwide temporary moratoria on provider enrollment across Medicare, Medicaid, and CHIP programs. Two separate announcements indicate significant scope.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Provider enrollment freezes will impact Texas's managed care operations and may require contract modifications to address network adequacy issues.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review impact on TX managed care networks and brief HHSC immediately

executiveInsights.title: Texas enrollment decline of -3.8% matches national trend post-unwinding
executiveInsights.summary: Texas lost 3.8% enrollment YoY, similar to national patterns, with current enrollment at 4M. Decline is moderate compared to MI (-7.6%) but consistent across large states.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Enrollment stabilization suggests redetermination process is completing, allowing HHSC to focus on operational improvements rather than crisis management.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Schedule strategic planning session with HHSC on post-unwinding priorities

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment decline pressure
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Medicaid enrollment declining 3.8% YoY creates budget pressures and operational adjustments for HHSC systems.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop enrollment forecasting models to help HHSC optimize capacity planning and budget allocation.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality improvement initiative
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks #30 nationally with 62/100 quality score, presenting significant improvement opportunities.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose comprehensive quality improvement program targeting Texas's low-performing metrics to reach top quartile.

riskOpportunity.label: Managed care recompete
riskOpportunity.description: High 93.3% managed care penetration in Texas signals potential for large-scale procurement opportunities.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Monitor HHSC procurement portal actively and prepare competitive positioning for managed care contract renewals.

riskOpportunity.label: Federal regulatory shifts
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple pending CMS regulations on community engagement and state directed payments create compliance uncertainty.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish dedicated regulatory monitoring team to provide HHSC with early compliance guidance and implementation support.

riskOpportunity.label: Provider enrollment moratorium
riskOpportunity.description: CMS temporary moratoriums on provider enrollment may disrupt network adequacy and access initiatives.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop alternative provider network strategies and waiver request templates for HHSC to maintain access standards.

riskOpportunity.label: HHS OIG oversight exposure
riskOpportunity.description: Active OIG Medicaid portfolio creates audit risk but also opportunities for proactive compliance consulting.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Position Accenture as the preferred compliance and audit readiness partner through proactive risk assessments.

riskOpportunity.label: Technology modernization wave
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple procurement opportunities visible on Texas ESBD suggest major system refresh cycles approaching.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Accelerate relationship building with HHSC IT leadership and prepare comprehensive modernization proposals.

riskOpportunity.label: Per-enrollee cost efficiency
riskOpportunity.description: Texas $3,014 per-enrollee cost above national average creates pressure for cost optimization initiatives.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop comprehensive cost reduction roadmap focusing on administrative efficiency and care management optimization.
